Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Understand the Users Request Input Part 1 Normative Process Behavior Strict Rules describes a loan application process with a fixed sequence and conditional branching Part 2 Desiderata Recommended Best Practices describes timing constraints and optional activities Event Log 3 cases with timestamps and activities Task 1 Identify cases violating normative rules strict and explain why 2 Identify cases failing desiderata conditions clarifying they are suboptimal but not severe 3 Suggest interpretations justifications for observed behaviors

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Normative Rules Part 1 Sequence Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Inform Applicant If Approved Finalize Documentation Close Case Ordering Rules Credit Check before Risk Assessment Risk Assessment before Loan Decision Inform Applicant after Loan Decision If Approved Finalize Documentation before Close Case If Rejected Do NOT finalize documentation before closing Not Allowed Skipping changing order contradictory actions finalize after rejection closing without decision logic

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Analyze Event Log against Normative Rules Case 1 App Received 0900 Credit Check 0930 Risk Assessment 1000 Loan Decision Approved 1100 Inform Applicant 1300 Finalize Documentation 1330 Close Case 1400 Sequence matches exactly Approved Finalize Close All good Case 2 App Received 0800 Risk Assessment 0820 Credit Check 0900 Loan Decision Rejected 0930 Finalize Documentation 1000 Close Case 1030 Violation 1 Risk Assessment 0820 happens BEFORE Credit Check 0900 Violates Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment Violation 2 Loan Decision is Rejected but Finalize Documentation 1000 occurs Violates If loan is rejected do not finalize documentation before closing the case Case 3 App Received 0900 Credit Check 14th 1000 Risk Assessment 1100 Loan Decision Approved 1200 Inform Applicant 16th 1500 Finalize Documentation 17th 0900 Close Case 1000 Sequence matches exactly Approved Finalize Close All normative rules met Conclusion for Task 1 Only Case 2 violates normative rules

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Desiderata Part 2 Recommended Credit Check within 24h of Application Received Recommended Inform Applicant within 48h of Loan Decision Encouraged Follow up Call before closing Failing these is not severe just suboptimal

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Check Event Log against Desiderata Case 1 App Received Jan 10 0900 Credit Check Jan 10 0930 Diff 30 mins Loan Decision Jan 10 1100 Inform Applicant Jan 10 1300 Diff 2 hours Follow up Call Not mentioned Case 2 App Received Jan 11 0800 Credit Check Jan 11 0900 Diff 1 hour Loan Decision Jan 11 0930 Inform Applicant Not in log Wait the log for Case 2 jumps from Loan Decision 0930 to Finalize Documentation 1000 It completely misses Inform Applicant But Inform Applicant is a normative rule so it is a normative violation not just desideratum Follow up Call Not logged Case 3 App Received Jan 12 0900 Credit Check Jan 14 1000 Diff about 49 hours Violates desideratum Loan Decision Jan 14 1200 Inform Applicant Jan 16 1500 Diff about 51 hours Violates desideratum Follow up Call Explicitly noted as No Follow up Call Violates desideratum

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Check Case 2 I noticed Inform Applicant is completely missing from Case 2s log The normative rule says Inform Applicant must occur after Loan Decision So Case 2 actually has three normative violations 1 Risk Assessment before Credit Check 2 Missing Inform Applicant skipping a step 3 Finalize Documentation after Rejection I must explicitly state this

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Task 3 Interpretations Justifications Case 2 Normative Violations Risk Assessment before Credit Check Could be a system glitch parallel processing error or manual override due to urgent request Maybe the credit check was initiated concurrently but logged later Finalize Documentation after Rejection Could be administrative error template preparation done prematurely or misclassification of the decision Strict rules say not allowed so its a process deviation needing correction Case 3 Desideratum Violations Delayed Credit Check and Inform Applicant High workload resource constraints system latency or applicant unavailability for follow up Since these are desiderata delays are acceptable but suboptimal Missing Follow up Call Staffing shortage automated process used instead or deemed unnecessary after approval General Process mining event logs often capture system generated vs human entries Timing constraints are guidelines for efficiency customer satisfaction not hard stops

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Structure the Response Follow the 3 tasks explicitly Use clear headings bullet points Reference specific timestamps and activities Distinguish clearly between normative strict and desideratum recommended violations Provide reasonable justifications interpretations Ensure tone is analytical and professional

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Timing Calculations Case 3 Credit Check Jan 12 0900 to Jan 14 1000 equals 49 hours The prompt says more than 24 hours later which is correct Inform Applicant Decision Jan 14 1200 to Jan 16 1500 equals 51 hours Correct Clarify Desideratum vs Normative Make sure the distinction is crystal clear Normative process invalid broken Desideratum process valid but inefficient suboptimal
